findings.title: annualBalance silently coerces missing debt components to 0
findings.description: When mapping annual balance-sheet rows from quoteSummary, `totalDebt = (std || 0) + (ltd || 0)` is used. Only the case where BOTH `std` and `ltd` are null returns null. If only one of the two debt fields is present (the common case for many non-US filers), the missing one is silently treated as $0, understating total debt.
findings.mechanism: Yahoo balanceSheetHistory returns `shortLongTermDebt` for some firms and `longTermDebt` for others. With the guard `(std == null && ltd == null) ? null : (std || 0) + (ltd || 0)`, any partial coverage falls back to 0 for the missing component instead of returning null or a partial-tag, so net-debt/EBITDA, EV/EBITDA, Altman-Z, ROIC are systematically understated for those tickers.
findings.reproducer: Mock yahoo input with `balanceSheetStatements: [{shortLongTermDebt: null, longTermDebt: 5e9, totalAssets: 50e9, cash: 1e9}]` then call mapYahooToCanonical. The output annualBalance[0].totalDebt is 5e9 (treated as $5B = total debt) but std component is unknown — should be flagged as partial.
findings.impact: Tickers with missing one debt component score higher on net-debt/EBITDA and Altman-Z than they should; survivor bias toward apparent-low-leverage firms in Premium-Compounder/Quality picks.
findings.suggested_fix: Either set totalDebt = null when either component is null (strict), or carry a `_debtPartial: true` flag in the row so downstream methods can refuse to compute. Same fix applies to `mapFTSToBalance` at line 479 which has identical pattern.
findings.compound_hint: Same `|| 0` pattern repeats at line 479 in mapFTSToBalance — FTS path is even more error-prone because FTS often returns one of the two fields. Cross-check with state-management agent if any downstream method assumes debt=0 means zero-leverage.

findings.title: Unknown currency keeps local values but advertises them as USD elsewhere
findings.description: _convertSnapshotToUSD: if a currency is missing from FX_TO_USD it returns the snapshot unmodified with `fxConversionFailed=true`, but `meta.reportingCurrency` is NOT overwritten to 'USD'. Good. BUT marketCap is left in local units while the downstream $1B MIN_MCAP filter on line 748 compares `canonical.marketCap.value < 1e9` directly assuming USD — so for tickers in unknown currencies like ARS/EGP/VND a billion-local-units cap admits or excludes by raw local-currency amount.
findings.mechanism: FX_FALLBACK lacks ARS, EGP, VND, NGN, CLP, COP, PHP, MYR, HUF, CZK, TWD-quoted dual listings, etc. For any of these `rate == null` path triggers, FX conversion is skipped, mcap stays in local currency, but the $1B USD floor is applied to the local-currency number. A Buenos Aires stock with mcap of 5B ARS (~$5M USD) sails through the >$1B floor.
findings.reproducer: watchlist entry with yahoo currency='ARS' and marketCap=2e9 (ARS, ~$2M USD). MIN_MCAP filter passes because 2e9 > 1e9, snapshot is written, ranked alongside genuine $2B USD firms.
findings.impact: Silent inclusion of micro-cap foreign tickers; cross-currency ratio noise; ranking corruption when sector medians mix USD and unconverted local values.
findings.suggested_fix: When fxConversionFailed=true, either (a) reject the snapshot outright before the MIN_MCAP filter, or (b) record meta.reportingCurrency unchanged AND make the MIN_MCAP filter refuse to apply (skip-with-reason='fx-unknown'). Also extend FX_FALLBACK to cover currencies actually present in the watchlist.
findings.compound_hint: Cross-check with refresh-fx.js and the universe list for which currencies the watchlist actually contains. The FX_FALLBACK table is missing several G20 currencies.

findings.title: GBp/GBP key matching is case-sensitive and brittle
findings.description: `const isPence = origCurrency === 'GBp'` requires an exact case-sensitive match. Yahoo has historically returned 'GBp', 'GBX', 'GBpence' and sometimes 'GBP' for LSE tickers depending on the exchange and listing class. If Yahoo returns 'GBX' or capitalised 'GBP' for an LSE stock that actually trades in pence, the divide-by-100 step is skipped — marketCap and financial series end up 100x too high.
findings.mechanism: LSE shares are quoted in pence (1/100 GBP) on Yahoo but the currency string is inconsistent. The normalizeRegion table maps both 'GBP' and 'GBp' to UK, but _convertSnapshotToUSD only triggers the pence-correction on the literal string 'GBp'. Drift in the upstream label silently produces 100x overstated UK marketCaps which sail through the $1B floor regardless of true size.
findings.reproducer: Mock pr.currency='GBX' and sd.marketCap=5e9 (pence). _convertSnapshotToUSD treats this as 5B GBP → ~$6.35B USD; true mcap is $63.5M.
findings.impact: Existing UK universe could contain 100x-overstated marketCaps for any ticker where Yahoo deviates from 'GBp'; these dominate UK sector medians and qualify for picks they shouldn't.
findings.suggested_fix: Treat any of GBP/GBp/GBX/GBPENCE as the pence path when the exchange is LSE/AIM and marketCap looks ≥100x peer median. Or normalize upstream: detect pence by checking `exchangeName ~ /london|lse|aim/i` + raw price magnitude vs GBP-listed siblings. Document the canonical list of known Yahoo pence labels.
findings.compound_hint: Refer to refresh-universe.js or LSE-specific data; the threshold-audit script may already have observed 100x outliers among LSE picks.

findings.title: FX hardcoded fallback is silently used without alerting downstream
findings.description: If fx-rates.json is missing, stale (>14d) or malformed, FX_SOURCE silently stays at 'fallback-hardcoded' with rates from 2024. Only a console.log message indicates this — no flag is written into any snapshot. Downstream consumers (sector medians, comparison vs picks history) cannot detect that the values are 2024-dollars-vs-2026-yen.
findings.mechanism: FX_FALLBACK is initialised in 2024 (JPY=0.0067, KRW=0.00074, EUR=1.08). FX has moved materially since then (JPY ~0.0064, EUR ~1.07). When refresh-fx.js fails (network, GitHub secret expiry), the loadFx IIFE prints a console line and pull continues. No per-snapshot meta.fxRateApplied annotation flags whether the fallback or the live table was used.
findings.reproducer: Delete fx-rates.json (or backdate fetchedAt to 30d ago). Run pull-yahoo on a EUR ticker. Check the resulting snapshot — meta.fxRateApplied is 1.08 with no indicator this was hardcoded.
findings.impact: Multi-month stale FX silently propagates into ratios and rankings, with no alert mechanism. Sector medians for EU/JP/UK skew over months without anyone noticing.
findings.suggested_fix: Set snap.meta.fxRateSource = FX_SOURCE in _convertSnapshotToUSD (currently only fxRateApplied is set). field-coverage.js or a dedicated alert should flag when >5% of snapshots have fxRateSource='fallback-hardcoded'.
findings.compound_hint: Cross-check refresh-fx.js failure handling and whether the workflow's quality gate inspects fxSource. Combined with F-DQ-002, FX failures are doubly silent.

findings.title: field-coverage.js does not detect zero-coverage as drift on cold start
findings.description: computeBaseline returns {} when history has fewer than 2 entries; detectDrift then skips any field where baseline[field] is undefined. So if a Yahoo field that was always 100% covered suddenly drops to 0% on the first run after a schema change, AND the alert-state.json was reset/deleted (e.g. after a CI cache wipe or after the Tag 153 path-correction commit), drift is silently never reported until 2 more runs accumulate. There is no absolute-floor check (e.g. 'any tracked field with <50% coverage on a single run should also raise an alarm').
findings.mechanism: Drift detection is purely relative to its own rolling baseline. After history truncation/reset, a 0% field looks like 'no baseline yet' rather than 'critical data missing'. The MIN_HISTORY_FOR_ALERT=2 gate compounds this.
findings.reproducer: Set alert-state.fieldCoverage history to []. Run with snapshots where annual.annualOpInc is empty for all tickers. detectDrift returns []. Engine continues to score all tickers using stale annual.annualOpInc.
findings.impact: On the first run after a schema-drift detection reset, complete field outages go undetected. Yahoo's Nov-2024 incomeStatementHistoryQuarterly outage (referenced in the file header) would not be detected on a fresh state.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an absolute floor: any tracked field with currentCoverage < 0.50 should always raise an alert regardless of baseline state. Or, on cold start with no baseline, log an INFO line listing zero-coverage fields so the operator at least sees them.
findings.compound_hint: field-coverage history lives in alert-state.json; cross-check state-management agent's findings for any path where alert-state is reset or corrupted.

findings.title: Coverage tracker omits FCF-margin, insider, IPO and pe-forward fields used by methods
findings.description: TRACKED_FIELDS covers 14 fields, but methods reference many more that are NOT tracked: metrics.fcfMarginTTM (used by fcf-yield, rule-of-40, premium-compounder), metrics.forwardPE (forward-pe method), metrics.insidersOwnership (insider-ownership), insiderActivity (insider-buy-cluster), annual.annualSBC (sbc-revenue), annual.annualCapex (capex-trend, reinvestment-rate), annual.annualBalance (net-debt-ebitda, altman-z), meta.firstTradeDate (used for IPO-year filters), and timeseries.netIncomeQ (quarterly-earnings-stability).
findings.mechanism: If Yahoo silently stops returning, say, freeCashflow, the entire FCF-Yield + Rule-of-40 + Premium-Compounder pipeline returns garbage values but field-coverage drift won't fire because metrics.fcfMarginTTM isn't tracked.
findings.reproducer: Mock fd.freeCashflow=null across all tickers. computeCoverage reports unchanged. detectDrift returns []. fcf-yield method receives null fcfMarginTTM and emits 0 / NEAR_MISS without any alert.
findings.impact: Schema-drift detection has blind spots in exactly the fields most exposed to Yahoo API churn (cash flow, insider, share count).
findings.suggested_fix: Extend TRACKED_FIELDS to include all fields listed in CRITICAL_FIELDS of methods/data-quality.js, plus metrics.fcfMarginTTM, metrics.insidersOwnership, annual.annualSBC, annual.annualCapex, annual.annualBalance, insiderActivity.buyCount90d, meta.firstTradeDate.
findings.compound_hint: Cross-check methods-engine agent for the full list of metric paths referenced; align TRACKED_FIELDS with CRITICAL_FIELDS to keep a single source of truth.

findings.title: Grade weights don't actually match the documented banding
findings.description: Docstring says 'Total weight = 12.5' but actual sum of CRITICAL_FIELDS weights is 10 critical-weighted-1.0 + the rest. Counting: 10 fields of weight 1.0 (lines 23-32) but line 33 annualOpInc>=3 is weight 0.5, and the list has only 9 critical-1.0 entries (sector/industry/ticker/marketCap/revTTM/revGrowthYoY/grossMargin/operatingMargin/fcfMarginTTM/annualRev>=3 = 10). Then 5 weight-0.5 entries = 2.5. Total = 12.5. Math checks out. BUT: ratio uses missingWeight / TOTAL_WEIGHT (12.5). The thresholds are doc'd as 'percent of FIELDS missing' but actually compute 'percent of WEIGHT missing'. A snapshot missing all 5 light fields (0.5*5=2.5/12.5=20%) is graded B even though 5/15 = 33% of fields are missing.
findings.mechanism: The grade banding (A: ≤10%, B: ≤30%, C: ≤50%) is documented as 'nanRatio = fehlende / total kritische Felder' but the implementation is weighted ratio. The thresholds are calibrated against the weighted denominator (12.5) not the unweighted count (15). This causes the actual B grade to be looser than documented.
findings.reproducer: Snapshot with all top-10 critical fields present and all 5 weight-0.5 annual/timeseries arrays empty: missingWeight = 2.5, ratio = 0.20 → B. The doc string implies B requires 'all top-10 plus 1-2 of the lighter fields' but in practice all 5 light fields can be missing while staying within B.
findings.impact: Grade B is broader than operators expect; tier-cap logic (B = no cap) admits picks whose history data is largely absent (no quarterly opInc, no FCF history, no balance-sheet, no GP).
findings.suggested_fix: Either re-calibrate thresholds based on weighted total (e.g. A: ≤0.08, B: ≤0.20, C: ≤0.40) and update docstring to say 'weighted nanRatio', OR change formula to use unweighted count `missing.length / CRITICAL_FIELDS.length` to match the docstring intent.
findings.compound_hint: Affects DATAQUALITY_ENFORCE downstream behavior in score-aggregator.js — overly lenient B grade means data-quality gate is mostly inactive in practice.

findings.title: Grade B has no tier cap; documented intent says 'C+' cap
findings.description: Docstring at line 12 reads: 'B: 10–30% fehlend — Tier-Cap C+ zulässig' (meaning B should be capped at C+). But tierCapForGrade returns null for grade B (line 100), i.e. no cap is applied. This is a direct contradiction between intent and implementation.
findings.mechanism: score-aggregator.js line 207 only triggers downgrade when cap === 'NEAR_MISS' or 'REJECT'. With tierCapForGrade('B') === null, B-graded stocks (10-30% data missing) score and tier exactly like A-graded.
findings.reproducer: Snapshot graded B (e.g. revenueGrowthYoY missing + 2 light fields missing). computeScore with DATAQUALITY_ENFORCE=1 returns A tier without dataQualityCapped=true. Compare with docstring expectation.
findings.impact: The data-quality enforcement is effectively only firing at grade C and D, meaning ~85% of partially-incomplete snapshots receive no penalty.
findings.suggested_fix: Decide canonical behavior: either update docstring to say 'B: no cap' or set tierCapForGrade('B') to return a sentinel like 'C_PLUS' and add the corresponding clamp in score-aggregator. Either way, doc and code must agree.
findings.compound_hint: Same code is also referenced by generate-modes-report.js and methodology-report.js — the user-facing dashboards may render the grade column with a misleading 'data-quality enforcement' badge.

findings.title: Stale-snapshot price-only fast-path freezes fundamentals indefinitely
findings.description: Tag-166 price-only fast-path: when an existing snapshot is < FUNDAMENTALS_MAX_AGE_DAYS (default 7) old, only price/marketCap are refreshed via yf.quote(). Fundamentals (annual, timeseries, metrics, insiderActivity, _quality grade) are preserved verbatim from the previous snapshot. The _quality grade is also NOT re-evaluated. If the universe runs more than once per FUNDAMENTALS_MAX_AGE_DAYS window, the 'fresh' snapshots actually carry month-old fundamentals.
findings.mechanism: _priceOnlyUpdate writes existing.meta.asOf = new Date().toISOString() but does not re-run gradeSnapshot or update meta.fxRateApplied. A snapshot that had _quality.grade='A' two weeks ago remains A even if its underlying field set is now stale. Sector-median lookups use meta.asOf to decide freshness windows; they will be misled.
findings.reproducer: Run pull-yahoo with FUNDAMENTALS_MAX_AGE_DAYS=7. Day 0: full pull, snapshot has revenueTTM=$100B, asOf=2026-01-01. Day 3: price-only path. asOf becomes 2026-01-04 but revenueTTM still says $100B. Downstream methods believe the data is 3 days old when fundamentals are 3 days old + revenueTTM is whatever was true at Q3 reporting.
findings.impact: Earnings-stability, revenue-shock-guard, q-spike-dataguard, deceleration-guard all depend on quarterly trends. Price-only snapshots can mask a real Q-over-Q drop until the next full pull. Survivor bias: tickers that lose data (Yahoo stops returning for them) keep their previous _quality.grade='A' via cached snapshot, even though no fresh data arrived.
findings.suggested_fix: (a) Re-run gradeSnapshot(existing) at the end of _priceOnlyUpdate to revalidate the grade against the (still-stale) fundamentals + current asOf. (b) Add a meta.fundamentalsAsOf field, kept separately from meta.asOf, that records when fundamentals were last freshly pulled — methods can then use it explicitly. (c) Cap consecutive price-only updates: after N price-only refreshes force a full pull regardless of age.
findings.compound_hint: Cross-check sector-median lookups and earnings-stability tests; both implicitly assume meta.asOf reflects fundamentals freshness.

findings.title: Mcap filter deletes existing snapshots without logging the deletion list
findings.description: When a previously-passing ticker drops below $1B USD mcap (or its FX conversion silently misfires per F-DQ-002), the existing snapshot is fs.unlinkSync()'d. Only an INFO log line is emitted, and the result is pushed with status='skipped-mcap'. No persistent record (a 'graveyard.json' or a 'removed' entry in manifest) is kept to support survivor-bias diagnosis later.
findings.mechanism: Universe silently shrinks on a noisy boundary (a 4% intraday move on a $1.05B stock can flip it below the floor). Downstream picks-history then loses the ticker's trajectory; backtest-from-history and walk-forward-perf cannot compute returns for tickers that left the universe because no marker is preserved.
findings.reproducer: Inject a stock with marketCap=1.01e9. Pull once → snapshot saved. Inject marketCap=0.99e9. Pull again → snapshot deleted. No history of why.
findings.impact: Survivor bias in any retrospective method-effectiveness measurement: tickers that dropped below the floor (often the worst performers!) disappear silently, biasing picks-vs-fail comparisons upward.
findings.suggested_fix: Before fs.unlinkSync, append a record to outputs/_removed.jsonl with {ticker, removedAt, lastMcap, reason}. Add to slim manifest a `removed_below_mcap` counter. Optionally, keep the snapshot file with a meta.belowMcapAt timestamp so picks-history retains the trail.
findings.compound_hint: Compounds with F-DQ-002 (unknown-currency tickers may slip in/out of the universe on the same boundary). State-management/CI agents may already have findings about universe shrinkage.

findings.title: Mcap-skip reason message hardcodes obsolete $2B/$300B thresholds
findings.description: MIN_MCAP is 1e9 and MAX_MCAP is Infinity (per the Tag 116 and Tag 101 comments), but the log message at line 749 still reads '< $2B (Small-Cap)' and '> $300B (Mega-Cap)'. Misleading diagnostics during postmortems.
findings.mechanism: String literals were not updated when constants changed. Operator triaging skipped tickers reads `mcap=0.95B < $2B (Small-Cap)` and assumes the threshold is $2B rather than $1B.
findings.reproducer: Inject a watchlist entry with marketCap=0.5e9. The log shows '< $2B (Small-Cap)' even though MIN_MCAP=1e9.
findings.impact: Wastes operator time during incident triage. Increases risk of subsequent mis-fixes when someone trusts the log rather than reading the code.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace static '$2B' / '$300B' with template-interpolated `$${(MIN_MCAP/1e9).toFixed(0)}B` and `$${(MAX_MCAP/1e9).toFixed(0)}B` (or 'no upper bound' when MAX_MCAP === Infinity).
findings.compound_hint: Trivial cosmetic fix but worth catching.

findings.title: No input digest / fingerprint exists for snapshots
findings.description: Each snapshot has meta.fetchedAt and meta.asOf, but no content-hash / digest of the canonical fields that downstream methods consume. There is no way to assert 'this snapshot's revenueTTM and annual.* are byte-identical to last run' beyond a full deep-equal comparison. Combined with F-DQ-009 (price-only path), this means stale fundamentals are not distinguishable from genuinely-unchanged-since-last-pull fundamentals.
findings.mechanism: Without a digest, deceleration-guard / revenue-shock-guard cannot quickly detect that a 'change' in revenueQ between two runs is due to actual new Yahoo data vs. a price-only update that preserved old fundamentals.
findings.reproducer: Compare two snapshots from consecutive runs — there is no _fingerprint field; only by hand-diffing the JSON can equivalence be established.
findings.impact: Diagnostic friction; cannot easily flag 'identical fundamentals across N consecutive runs' (a sign of Yahoo blacking out a ticker).
findings.suggested_fix: Add meta.fundamentalsFingerprint = sha1(JSON.stringify({annual, timeseries, metrics.revenueTTM, marketCap.value})) computed in the mapper, separate from meta.asOf. Methods can then detect 'this ticker has had the same fingerprint for 30 days → data may be frozen'.
findings.compound_hint: Light-touch nice-to-have; not blocking. Useful for the data-pipeline agent's stale-data detection.

findings.title: scripts/data-quality-report.js does not exist
findings.description: The scope of this audit listed scripts/data-quality-report.js as 'if it exists'. Confirmed: no such script exists in scripts/ or repo root. There is no standalone reporting tool that aggregates the per-snapshot _quality grades into a run-level summary (count by grade, list of D-grade tickers, comparison vs previous run).
findings.mechanism: _quality is written into each snapshot but no human-readable rollup is generated. Operators must hand-grep snapshots/*.json to see grade distribution.
findings.impact: No regression alarm if grade distribution shifts (e.g. 50% A → 50% C overnight from a Yahoo schema change).
findings.suggested_fix: Implement scripts/data-quality-report.js that loads all snapshots, counts by _quality.grade, lists top-20 missingFields by frequency, compares against the previous run, and writes to outputs/data-quality-report.json + .html. Run it as a workflow step after pull-yahoo and before commit.
findings.compound_hint: Combined with F-DQ-005 and F-DQ-006, the project relies on alert-state baseline drift only — there is no human-facing data-quality dashboard.
